items.name: window.MRR shared review editor (review.js)
items.detail: The single largest reusable unit. Manages state object S (rows, categories, totalPages, status, selected, watching, saveTimer, summaries, summaryPage, editingSummary) at review.js:16-30. Public handle window.MRR (review.js:930-944) = {loadDocument(id), getRows(), pollJob, enterEditor, setOnSummarize(fn), setOnReviewed(fn), setRowFilter(fn), api, banner, state}. Three pipeline steps toggled via show()/gotoStep (review.js:59-132): identify panel, review-&-correct table (rows table + same-origin PDF.js iframe at /static/vendor/pdfjs/web/viewer.html, review.js:12,265-300), summaries list. Row ops: inline edit/merge/split/delete/insert, AI 'suggest_merge' chips + bulk apply (review.js:451-587). Debounced autosave (800ms) only when valid; client validation mirrors server (review.js:304-347). Summaries: parseDisplay strips baked-in decoration tags for web view (review.js:627-640), in-place edit/exclude/resummarize/paginate (review.js:655-837). API calls: GET /api/documents/<id>, GET .../status, PUT .../rows, POST .../segment/start, POST .../summarize/start, GET .../summaries, PUT .../summaries/<idx>, POST .../summaries/<idx>/resummarize, POST .../export, GET .../pdf.

items.name: DocTable factory (doc-table.js + _doc_table.html)
items.detail: window.DocTable.create(config) with config {onOpen, onLoaded, onError, allowDelete}; returns {refresh, getDocs, state} (doc-table.js:125-350). Also exports DocTable.api (fetch wrapper w/ XSRF + 401->/login, doc-table.js:65-81) and DocTable.cookieValue. Renders status badges (STATUS_LABELS/STATUS_TONES doc-table.js:15-32), filter chips w/ counts, filename search, sortable headers (SORT_ACCESSORS:45-51), 20/page pagination, per-row overflow menu (Open / Start|Re-run identification / Delete). allowDelete:false hides Delete for bundle pickers (doc-table.js:107-123). Self-polls refresh every 2000ms while any doc has active_job (doc-table.js:235). API: GET /api/documents, POST .../segment/start, DELETE /api/documents/<id>.

items.name: My Documents page (home.html + home.js)
items.detail: Landing page at '/'. Purpose: list uploaded records, upload new ones, route into /review/<id>. Client flow: instantiates DocTable {onOpen -> window.location=/review/<id>, onLoaded -> toggle empty vs populated view (home.js:16-26)}. Upload via button/browse/drag-drop onto #dropZone or #tableCard; POSTs multipart to /api/documents; surfaces sha256_duplicate note; does NOT auto-start identification by design (home.js:38-83, comment at :4-5). Empty state = 3-step explainer + dropzone (home.html:41-74). API: POST /api/documents, GET /api/documents (via DocTable).

items.name: Review editor page (review.html + _doc_editor.html)
items.detail: Route /review/<document_id> (bare /review redirects to /, pages.py:56-66). Shell renders topbar, 3-state ev-stepper, the shared _doc_editor.html, a summaries section, and the export ev-dialog with 4 Word-header fields (patientName/patientdob/QMEorAME/lawfirm, review.html:74-109). <body data-doc-id> makes review.js auto-boot (review.js:9,948). Export downloads a .docx blob via POST /api/documents/<id>/export (review.js:875-905). This page = MRR engine + Summaries UI + export modal.

items.name: Diagnostics/Depositions bundle workspaces (bundle.html + bundle-page.js)
items.detail: Routes /diagnostics (categories ['3','8']) and /depositions (['9']) render one bundle.html with data-bundle-label/slug/categories (pages.py:72-89, bundle.html:13-15). NO data-doc-id so MRR does not auto-boot (bundle.html:11-12). Flow: DocTable picker (allowDelete:false) or upload -> selectDocument mounts shared MRR editor in place via MRR.loadDocument (bundle-page.js:56-72), hides the editor's own Summarize button, injects a 'Show only <label> documents' view-only category filter into .rc-actions (bundle-page.js:79-97), and via MRR.setOnReviewed shows a bundle-actions card. Actions download combined PDF or summarize-to-Word of matching-category docs. API: POST /api/documents/<id>/bundle/pdf, POST .../bundle/summarize (blob download w/ Content-Disposition filename parse, bundle-page.js:111-135).

items.name: Admin console (admin.html + admin.js)
items.detail: Route /admin (server is_admin-gated, pages.py:92-96; nav link shown only when current_user.is_admin in home/review/bundle templates). Category table (id/name/auto-assign/active/prompt-custom-or-general) + two ev-dialog modals: category metadata (id immutable on edit, name/description/examples/auto_assign/active) and summary-prompt editor (ev-mono textarea, shows custom vs inherited-general). API: GET/POST /api/admin/categories, PATCH /api/admin/categories/<id> (also used for activate/deactivate toggle), GET /api/admin/prompts/<id>, PUT /api/admin/prompts/<id> (admin.js:39-159). Names textContent-set to avoid injection (admin.js:64).

items.name: Login page (security/login_user.html + base.html)
items.detail: Flask-Security-Too render extending security/base.html. WTForm login_user_form (email/password/remember + hidden CSRF via hidden_tag()); posts to url_for_security('login'). Includes security/_messages.html (Flask-Security built-in partial, not present in repo). Styled with auth-* + ev-inp/ev-btn classes; per-field .invalid + auth-field-error rendering. Link to register.

items.name: Register page (security/register_user.html + register.js)
items.detail: Flask-Security render; WTForm register_user_form (name/email/password/optional password_confirm + hidden_tag CSRF); posts to url_for_security('register'). register.js drives a live checklist (8+ chars / number / symbol) and confirm-match, gating the submit button; server enforces identical rules (MrrPasswordUtil) so it is UX-only (register.js:1-45).

items.name: ev-dialog modal pattern
items.detail: Reusable modal: .ev-dialog-backdrop > .ev-dialog[role=dialog][aria-modal] with -head/-x/-sub/-row/-field-1/-field-2/-foot parts (evaluators.css:614-642, .ev-dialog-wide:688). Three instances: export dialog (review.html:74-109), category dialog + prompt dialog (admin.html:57-121). Behaviors (per-page JS): open/close by class toggle, backdrop-click close, Escape close, focus first field (review.js:845-873; admin.js:71-88). No shared modal JS - each page re-implements open/close.

items.name: Fetch + XSRF + 401 data-access pattern
items.detail: Every modern JS module repeats the same api() wrapper: fetch with Accept: application/json; on unsafe methods (method !== GET) add X-XSRF-Token header read from the XSRF-TOKEN cookie (cookieValue helper); opts.json serializes body + sets Content-Type; 401 -> window.location='/login'; non-ok -> throw Error(data.error). Duplicated in doc-table.js:60-81, review.js:85-107, admin.js:7-27, bundle-page.js:18-21, and inline in review.js export (875-891) / bundle-page.js download (111-118). Session is a Flask-Security cookie; XSRF is the cookie/header double-submit.

items.name: Job-progress polling pattern
items.detail: Two client polling loops stand in for push/queue: doc-table.js refreshes the whole list via setTimeout(refresh,2000) while any doc has active_job (doc-table.js:234-235); review.js pollDocument setInterval(GET /status,1000) drives the progress bar during segment/summarize, resolving on job.state done|error|interrupted and auto-advancing the stepper (review.js:183-241). active_job shape {kind, state, stage, current, total, error}; STAGE_LABELS at review.js:147-153.

items.name: Evaluators design system (evaluators.css tokens + classes)
items.detail: Tokens in :root (evaluators.css:7-64): navy-900..50, gold-700..100, blue-600/500/100, gray-50..700 + --ink, semantic success/warning/danger (500/100), role vars (--color-border, -focus, -text-muted, -text-on-navy), --shadow-focus/md/lg, --radius-sm/md/lg/pill, fluid --gutter clamp(20px,3.5vw,72px) + --content-cap 2560px, --font-heading Poppins / --font-body Inter. Component families: shell (ev-topbar/-crest-chip/-wordmark/-stepper/-step w/ active|done|busy states); controls (ev-btn +primary/outline/ghost/gold/del/sm/lg/block, ev-inp, ev-lbl, ev-cb, ev-check, ev-chip +review/edit/off); dialog (ev-dialog* family); pager (ev-pager); table/home (hd-column/-header/-toolbar/-chip/-search/-card/-table/-w-*/-doc/-name/-badge+tones/-dots/-menu/-foot/-empty/-drop/-steps); review editor (rc-column/-filename/-header/-status/-save+states/-actions/-title/-inp/-sel/-selwrap/-catfilter, add-form, editor-split, table-wrap, viewer-wrap, doc-row/title-row/gap-row, panel/center-panel, bar/bar-fill); summaries (sum-column/-header/-countline, summary-list/-card+excluded|editing, sum-heading/-title/-date/-text, summary-empty); auth (auth-main/-card/-head/-crest/-sub/-form/-field/-field-error/-remember/-alt/-alert/-checklist/-check+met|failed|unmet, ev-eyebrow). Vendored fonts at /static/vendor/fonts/fonts.css; crest at /static/assets/evaluators-crest.png.

items.name: Classic hub (index.html) + ancillary classic pages
items.detail: index.html (served /classic) plus pages.html, pagesManual.html, pdfsegment.html, checkCSV.html, DiagAndOpReports.html, DepositionReports.html, individual_mrr.html (served /pages, /checkCSV, etc. per pages.py:16-53). jQuery 3.6 + select2 from CDNs, inline <script> blocks, hit the single-user state.py-globals routes (/upload, /uploadPages, /summarize, /reset, /getpatientnameanddob, /getlawfirm, /getPages, /segmentPDF, /exportresultstoword, etc.). No per-user document model, no XSRF pattern. Superseded by the modern per-document flow.

portingNotes: Data-fetching: replace the four hand-rolled api() wrappers with one typed client (fetch or TanStack Query). The XSRF double-submit (read XSRF-TOKEN cookie -> X-XSRF-Token header on unsafe methods) and the 401->/login redirect must be reproduced against whatever session/CSRF scheme the FastAPI back-end adopts; if FastAPI keeps Flask-Security's cookie+CSRF the client logic ports 1:1, otherwise this becomes the main auth rewrite.

Polling -> live state: both polling loops (2s list poll in doc-table.js, 1s /status poll in review.js) are natural TanStack Query refetchInterval / SWR candidates keyed on active_job presence. This is also the seam where the externalized Redis job queue lands (Path B): the /status contract {kind,state,stage,current,total,error} can stay identical, so the React progress UI need not change if the back-end swaps the job source.

window.MRR is the central decomposition problem: it is one module doing three steps + a mutable global S + a host-injection API (setOnSummarize/setOnReviewed/setRowFilter/loadDocument). In React it becomes a <ReviewEditor> with a state store (Zustand/Context or useReducer over the S shape) and props/callbacks replacing the window.MRR handle; the bundle page's 'mount editor in place and re-point at a new doc' becomes routing/remount, and setRowFilter/setOnReviewed become props. Preserve the exact autosave-only-when-valid and client validation (rowErrors mirrors server) semantics.

DocTable is a clean factory -> <DocTable onOpen onLoaded onError allowDelete/> React component; STATUS_LABELS/TONES, FILTERS, SORT_ACCESSORS become module constants; keep textContent-based filename/name rendering (PHI-safe, doc-table.js:204, admin.js:64) as React auto-escaping already handles it.

Design tokens -> theme: lift the :root custom properties verbatim into a global CSS layer (they already work in React) or generate a Tailwind theme from them (colors, spacing, radii, fontFamily). The fluid --gutter clamp and --content-cap are layout primitives to keep. Component classes (ev-*/hd-*/rc-*/sum-*/auth-*) should map to a component library (CSS Modules / Tailwind @apply / styled), not be copied as global selectors, since the vanilla markup builds them via innerHTML strings.

PDF viewer: review.js embeds the vendored PDF.js viewer in an iframe and drives it via contentWindow.PDFViewerApplication.page for row-click page jumps (review.js:265-300). React needs an equivalent programmatic-page viewer (react-pdf, or keep the vendored pdf.js in an iframe with a typed postMessage/ref bridge). Chrome's built-in viewer was explicitly rejected because it ignores #page after first load.

Blob downloads (export .docx, bundle pdf/docx) use manual anchor.click()+URL.revokeObjectURL and parse Content-Disposition for the filename (review.js:893-898, bundle-page.js:120-131). Reproduce in a small download helper; these are POST-with-JSON-body -> binary responses, not simple links.

Server-rendered auth: login/register are Flask-Security WTForm renders with url_for_security() actions and a hidden_tag() CSRF field, plus an included _messages.html that ships with Flask-Security (not in this repo). In Next these become client forms POSTing to the FastAPI auth endpoints; the register live-checklist (register.js) ports directly to controlled inputs. Confirm the target auth stack before porting - this is the least framework-agnostic surface.

Decoration parsing lives in the client (parseDisplay strips [ManualCheck]/[Diagnostic Study]/(Pages X-Y)/**DOI** from stored strings for display, review.js:627-640) while the Word export recomposes them server-side. Keep this split: the React summary card reuses parseDisplay logic; do not move it server-side or the export format breaks.

The classic pages and styles.css are drop: they depend on state.py process-global single-user session, which the port abandons. Do not port index.html/pages/pdfsegment/checkCSV/DiagAndOpReports/DepositionReports/individual_mrr or the /upload,/summarize,/reset,/getPages,/getpatientnameanddob,/getlawfirm,/exportresultstoword,/segmentPDF routes they call.
risks: window.MRR carries hidden coupling via mutable module state and host-injected callbacks (onSummarize/onReviewed/rowFilter/DOC_ID). A naive component split can drop edge cases: the 'don't navigate while S.watching' guard (review.js:113), reset-on-reload (loadDocument/resetEditorState review.js:907-926), and the bundle page hiding the editor's own Summarize button (bundle-page.js:60-62).

Both bundle and review load review.js AND the bundle page loads doc-table.js + bundle-page.js as separate global scripts sharing window; the current split relies on load order (review.js defines window.MRR before bundle-page.js runs, bundle.html:93-95). React module imports remove this ordering hazard but the porter must re-establish the same init sequencing (table.refresh only after MRR is ready).

PDF.js is a vendored same-origin iframe with a private API (PDFViewerApplication). If the React stack changes origins/CSP or uses a hosted viewer, the programmatic page-jump breaks silently (the try/catch at review.js:290-298 swallows the failure and falls back to a full reload).

Admin nav visibility is driven by a Jinja {% if current_user.is_admin %} at render time (home/review/bundle templates); in a client-rendered React app this gate must move to an authenticated /whoami-style call (admin_api.py:29 exposes /api/admin/whoami) or the Admin link/route leaks to non-admins even though the API stays gated.

The four fetch/XSRF wrappers are duplicated with subtle differences (doc-table.js scopes to absolute URLs, review.js prefixes /api/documents/<DOC_ID>, admin.js uses body-not-string detection). Consolidating them risks changing the base-URL assumptions each page depends on.

PHI: the export/bundle dialogs collect patient name/DOB and the summaries render medical text; the React UI must keep the PHI-safe textContent rendering and must not log request/response bodies or place PHI in URLs (the note text at review.js:851 explicitly warns users not to add extra PHI).
openQuestions: Will the FastAPI back-end keep Flask-Security's session-cookie + XSRF-TOKEN double-submit scheme, or move to a token/JWT scheme? This decides whether the entire client auth/XSRF layer ports 1:1 or is rewritten, and whether login/register stay server-rendered or become client forms.

Next.js rendering mode for these pages: are they client-rendered SPA routes (closest to current behavior, since all data comes from JSON APIs) or SSR/RSC with server data fetching? The polling/autosave/interactive-editor nature argues for client components, but the admin gate and initial doc list could be server-fetched.
Is the vendored PDF.js viewer being kept as-is (iframe) in the port, or replaced with a React PDF component? The row-click page-jump contract depends on the answer.

Should the design system be delivered as raw CSS-var + component CSS (fastest, reuses evaluators.css nearly verbatim) or refactored into a Tailwind theme / component library? Impacts how ev-*/hd-*/rc-* classes are reproduced.

The included security/_messages.html is a Flask-Security built-in template not present in the repo - confirm the target keeps Flask-Security-style flash/message rendering or replaces it with the app's standard {data,meta,errors} envelope surfaced as toasts.

Confirm the classic pages are truly retired for the port (drop) and no user workflow still depends on /classic, /pages, /pdfsegment, /checkCSV, /DiagAndOpReports, /DepositionReports, /IndividualMRR - the index.html header still links all of them.

result.summary: The services/ layer is deliberately Flask-free business logic (the "no Flask imports" rule is documented in mrr_ai/services/CLAUDE.md), so the bulk of it ports to FastAPI unchanged: PDF/OCR/window/segment/verify/summarize/bundle logic all take plain args and return plain data, reading only config constants and the pre-built genai_client. The GenAI clients and config are the seams: extensions.py mixes Flask-SQLAlchemy db with a framework-agnostic google-genai client builder (Vertex/BAA vs Developer-API routing) plus a legacy OpenAI client; config.py holds all env vars, model names, caps and fail-fast REQUIRED_ENV validation and moves nearly verbatim. The ONE sanctioned exception to service purity is classification.py lazily reading the editable DB catalog via mrr_ai.catalog (falls back to taxonomy.py constants when there is no app context) - that read couples to Flask-SQLAlchemy and must be re-seated on a FastAPI DB session. prompts.py and taxonomy.py are pure seed/fallback constants and port as-is. The classic single-user surface (state.py globals, services/jobs.py single-slot runner, categorization.py legacy difflib matcher) is drop; job_queue.py is the in-process pool that becomes the external Redis/worker redesign. PHI flows: multi-user PDFs land at UPLOAD_FOLDER/<user_id>/<uuid>.pdf (uuid names keep patient filenames out of paths/logs), OCR text is held in memory or persisted as Summary.source_text (fine-tuning input = PHI in DB), and what reaches Vertex is inline PDF window bytes (segment), page PNGs + OCR excerpts (verify), title + first-page OCR (classify), and full row OCR text (summarize); embeddings stay local; logging/audit is ids-only. The full pipeline must run on the Vertex BAA path (GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI=true); the OpenAI legacy path is non-BAA/unfunded and only the classic routes use it.

files.role: Authoritative map of services/ + the 'no Flask imports' purity rule and the one classification catalog exception
Core sliding-window segmentation pipeline (Flask-free); inline PDF bytes -> Vertex -> tiled categorized rows
B5 categorization cascade; the ONE sanctioned Flask/DB coupling (lazy catalog read)
Boundary verification (page PNGs + OCR excerpts -> Vertex); PHI-carrying
Per-row summarization (row OCR text -> Vertex); persists sourceText fine-tuning input
Client seam: genai_client (Vertex/Gemini) + legacy OpenAI client + Flask-SQLAlchemy db
All env vars, model names, caps, REQUIRED_ENV + fail-fast validate_env
Classic single-user mutable globals -> drop
Multi-user PDF upload PHI storage (UPLOAD_FOLDER/<user_id>/<uuid>.pdf)
Ids-only audit trail (PHI-redaction rule enforced here)

items.name: services/pdf.py
items.detail: pypdf helpers get_pdf_size (L10), get_pdf_page_count (L17), segment_pdf (L29, writes ~/MRRs), segment_pdf_locally (L66, writes UPLOAD_FOLDER). Flask-free; only imports config.UPLOAD_FOLDER. get_pdf_page_count still used by multi-user create_document (documents_api L93); the fixed-100-page segment_* funcs are classic-only and superseded by segment_engine.

items.name: services/ocr.py
items.detail: Tesseract via pdf2image/Poppler. extract_text_from_image (L14), extract_text_from_selected_pages (L23), extract_text_from_all_pages (L50). Honors config.TESSERACT_CMD override (L10-11). Flask-free; shells out to native binaries (mock in tests). Produces PHI text held in memory.

items.name: services/windows.py
items.detail: Pure byte-budgeted overlapping window packing: page_raw_sizes (L18), next_window_start (L36, overlap capped at 1/3), byte_budgeted_windows (L46, fails fast if a page exceeds budget). No Flask, no I/O beyond reading the PDF path.

items.name: services/gemini.py
items.detail: Data-only segmentation assets: PROMPT_VERSION='2' (L10), SEGMENTATION_SYSTEM (L12), SEGMENTATION_PROMPT (L18), SEGMENT_RESPONSE_SCHEMA (L60), tolerant parse_segment_item (L90). No client. INLINE PDF delivery only (Files API removed with Vertex port).

items.name: services/genai_retry.py
items.detail: generate_with_retry (L29): full-jitter exponential backoff over google.genai ServerError/transient-429/httpx.TransportError; re-raises non-429 and PerDay/free_tier. Client passed explicitly (patchable seam). Reads GENAI_MAX_RETRIES/BASE/MAX_DELAY from config. Flask-free.

items.name: services/segment_engine.py
items.detail: run_segmentation (L134): byte_budgeted_windows -> per-window inline PDF-bytes call to Vertex (_window_rows L53, types.Part.from_bytes) -> ownership merge_window_rows (L89, pure) -> ThreadPoolExecutor categorize (CLASSIFY_WORKERS) -> optional verify_and_merge. progress(stage,current,total) callback. Flask-free; imports genai_client + classify + verify + windows + gemini + ocr.

items.name: services/classification.py
items.detail: classify (L252) cascade: rules (L79) -> local sentence-transformers embeddings (L209, PHI stays local) -> Gemini constrained-enum llm_classify (L221). Sends title+first-page OCR text to Vertex only in LLM stage. THE Flask exception: lazy `from mrr_ai import catalog` reads (L131,L140) the DB catalog keyed on catalog_version, cached (L98-124), falling back to taxonomy.CATEGORIES when no app context. Thread-locked caches + _embed_lock.

items.name: services/verify_pass.py
items.detail: verify_and_merge (L168, suggest-mode default) + suspect_indices (L143, wide net capped by VERIFY_SUSPECT_CAP=200) + _same_document oracle (L89): sends prev-doc last page PNG + fragment page PNGs + (VERIFY_USE_TEXT) 1200-char boundary OCR excerpts to Vertex VERIFY_MODEL. Rasterize-once via _page_image (L45). Failure -> keep boundary. Flask-free.

items.name: services/summarize_engine.py
items.detail: summarize_row (L49): extract_text_from_selected_pages(row pages) -> Gemini SUMMARY_MODEL body (temp 0.8) + TITLE_PROMPT (temp 0.0). prompt injected by caller (DB-first) or falls back to prompts.py; category_11 KeyError guarded via prompts.get default (L62). Returns output_dict incl. sourceText = exact model input (PHI, persisted for fine-tuning). Flask/DB-free.

items.name: services/bundles.py
items.detail: matched_rows (L17), pages_for_rows (L23), build_bundle_pdf (L31, in-memory BytesIO pypdf concat, NO ~/MRRs artifacts), bundle_summary_entries (L49, reuses summarize_row with injected prompt_for resolver). BUNDLE_SUMMARIZE_CAP bounds sync path. Flask/DB-free.

items.name: services/files.py
items.detail: allowed_file (L10), safe_name (L14, wraps werkzeug.secure_filename for path-traversal defense), parse_date/is_valid_date (L24,L31), count_lines_in_file (L41). No Flask app context, but imports werkzeug.utils.secure_filename (Flask-ecosystem dep, not bundled by FastAPI/Starlette).

items.name: services/audit.py
items.detail: audit(action, document_id, user_id) (L11): ids-only AuditLog row, commits its own tx. Docstring (L1-5) states original filename is PHI and must NEVER reach the audit table or logs - log/audit by document id only. Lazily imports flask_security.current_user (L15) and uses extensions.db - coupled to Flask-Security + Flask-SQLAlchemy.

items.name: services/categorization.py
items.detail: Legacy difflib fuzzy matcher normalize/similarity/categorize_documents (L7,L11,L15). Superseded by classification.py and no longer called (per services/CLAUDE.md L32-33); kept pending removal.

items.name: services/jobs.py
items.detail: Single-slot in-memory background runner start/status/clear (L16,L53,L64) for the CLASSIC review UI; module-level _job + _lock, one case at a time. Superseded by job_queue.py in the document-scoped flow.

items.name: services/job_queue.py
items.detail: DB-backed queue on in-process ThreadPoolExecutor (PIPELINE_WORKERS=2). submit (L82), _run (L114, drives Document.status state machine - single writer), sweep_orphans (L53, boot reconciliation), init_app (L44). Uses flask current_app + app_context + scoped sessions. Explicit SINGLE-PROCESS constraint (L14-16, L114). This is the externalize-to-Redis+workers target.

items.name: extensions.genai_client (Vertex/Gemini)
items.detail: _build_client (L37): routes on config.USE_VERTEX; Vertex + GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT -> ADC (genai.Client(vertexai=True, project, location)); Vertex w/o project -> GEMINI_API_KEY against Vertex endpoint; else Developer API key. Built once at import after validate_env(). Builder logic is framework-agnostic; lives in a module that also defines Flask-SQLAlchemy db + SQLite WAL pragmas (L21-34).

items.name: extensions.client (OpenAI legacy)
items.detail: client = OpenAI() (L56), reads OPENAI_API_KEY from env. Only consumed by classic state-based routes: summarize.py (client.chat.completions L110,L129,L300,L316) and extraction.py (L18,L65). Non-BAA, account unfunded (config L57); superseded by Gemini summarize on Vertex.

items.name: Tesseract/Poppler OCR integration
items.detail: pytesseract + pdf2image.convert_from_path (ocr.py, verify_pass._page_image L47). TESSERACT_CMD override (config L24) for Windows PATH gaps. Native binaries must be present on FastAPI hosts AND on the externalized workers that run OCR-bearing stages.

items.name: config.py env/model/caps + validate_env
items.detail: load_dotenv (L7); REQUIRED_ENV=(GEMINI_API_KEY,OPENAI_API_KEY,SECRET_KEY,SECURITY_PASSWORD_SALT) (L9); UPLOAD_FOLDER=<repo>/uploads (L19); USE_VERTEX (L46); GENAI_MODEL default gemini-2.5-flash on Vertex (L52); SUMMARY_MODEL/VERIFY_MODEL (L58,L81); retry+window+worker+verify caps (L61-101); SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI SQLite (L35); validate_env fail-fast (L104). Pure Python.

items.name: prompts.py (seed constants)
items.detail: prompts dict of 14 per-category summary system prompts (category_01..category_14, category_100). Seed source for the Prompt DB table + runtime fallback (per mrr_ai/CLAUDE.md L22-26). category_11 absent -> summarize_engine falls back to category_100. No Flask. ~53KB of prompt text.

items.name: taxonomy.py (seed constants)
items.detail: Frozen Category dataclass + CATEGORIES dict (L36, ids 1-5,7-14,100; 6 omitted), ALLOWED_IDS (L227), DEFAULT_ID='100' (L230). Seed source for Category DB table + fallback used by classification when no app/DB context. Pure Python.

items.name: state.py globals
items.detail: Module-level mutable globals (pdf_filepath, txt_filepath, all_data, num_pages, patientNameGlobal, indiv_mrr_folder_path, etc.) for the CLASSIC single-process UI. Imported by 8 classic blueprints (export/extraction/individual_mrr/pages/reports/segmentation/upload/summarize). Contains a stale hardcoded path pdf_savepath='/home/usera/...' (L15). Root cause of the single-process constraint.

items.name: PHI-at-rest: multi-user PDF upload storage
items.detail: documents_api.create_document (L79-91): UPLOAD_FOLDER/<user_id>/<uuid>.pdf; uuid storage names keep patient filenames out of paths/process listings/logs (explicit comment L86-87). original_filename kept only in the Document DB row (L105). sha256 dedup warning (L98-100). This is the canonical PHI-at-rest convention to preserve.

items.name: PHI-at-rest: classic upload/export paths
items.detail: Classic routes write PATIENT-NAMED artifacts: upload.py UPLOAD_FOLDER/<filename> (L23-24), individual_mrr/reports uploads/<patient_folder> (individual_mrr L59-60), export.py + reports.py ~/MRRs/*.csv/*.docx/*.pdf (export L134,L163-166,L192; reports L24,L202). Filenames/folders derive from patient input - the PHI-in-path anti-pattern the multi-user flow eliminated.

items.name: PHI-in-transit: content sent to Vertex/Gemini
items.detail: Four PHI egress points to Vertex (BAA path only when USE_VERTEX): segment_engine _window_rows inline PDF window bytes (L61); verify_pass _same_document page PNGs + 1200-char boundary OCR (L125-127,L124); classification.llm_classify title+first-page OCR text (L227); summarize_engine full row OCR text (L38,L66). All via generate_with_retry(genai_client). HIPAA gate: GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI=true required for PHI (config L44-45).

items.name: PHI-in-transit: content sent to OpenAI (legacy)
items.detail: Classic summarize.py (L110,L129,L300,L316) and extraction.py (L18,L65) send OCR text of state.pdf_filepath pages to OpenAI chat.completions. Non-BAA endpoint - a PHI exposure that must NOT be carried to the port; superseded by the Vertex summarize path.

items.name: PHI-local: embedding classification stage
items.detail: classification.embed_classify (L209) runs sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2 locally; document title/OCR text is embedded on-box and never leaves the process (docstring L11-12, stage-2 comment). Only the LLM stage egresses text. Preserves a local-only PHI path worth keeping in the port + on workers.

items.name: PHI-in-memory: bundle assembly
items.detail: bundles.build_bundle_pdf (L31) concatenates source pages into an io.BytesIO buffer; bundle_summary_entries summarizes in-memory. Explicit no-~/MRRs-artifacts HIPAA choice (docstring L5-7); ids-only logging left to caller. Contrast with classic reports.py which writes merged PDFs to ~/MRRs.

items.name: PHI-at-rest: fine-tuning source text in DB
items.detail: summarize_engine returns sourceText = exact model input OCR text (L81-83), persisted to Summary.source_text (added additive column, __init__ L104). SegmentRow (raw model output) vs ReviewRow (human-corrected) is the fine-tuning dataset (mrr_ai/CLAUDE.md L10-11). PHI stored in the SQLite DB that the port KEEPS - re-review DB-at-rest protection.

items.name: Logging/redaction rule (ids-only)
items.detail: Redaction is by convention, not a central sink: audit.py enforces ids-only (docstring). Services use bare print() for progress/errors (page numbers, ids, exception strings) - e.g. segment_engine L127,L168; verify_pass L66,L138; classification L243,L273. Classic summarize.py prints state.pdf_filepath (L102,L292) - path leakage tolerable only because classic is dropped; the port needs a structured logger with a redaction boundary.

portingNotes: Moves to FastAPI unchanged (pure Flask-free Python): pdf.py, ocr.py, windows.py, gemini.py, genai_retry.py, segment_engine.py, verify_pass.py, summarize_engine.py, bundles.py, files.py (werkzeug.secure_filename must stay a dep or be swapped for an equivalent slugifier), and the seed constants prompts.py + taxonomy.py. These take plain args and return plain data - no request/app-context assumptions.

Client init under FastAPI: lift extensions._build_client (the USE_VERTEX / ADC-vs-API-key routing) verbatim into a module-level singleton or a lifespan-startup provider; it is framework-agnostic. Split extensions.py so `db` becomes a plain SQLAlchemy engine/session (the app keeps the same SQLite file + WAL/busy_timeout/foreign_keys pragmas via a connect event) instead of Flask-SQLAlchemy. Drop the OpenAI client entirely.

config.py ports as-is; keep load_dotenv + validate_env fail-fast at startup. Two edits implied by the port: remove OPENAI_API_KEY from REQUIRED_ENV (OpenAI path dropped), and treat GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI=true as a hard production requirement (currently only a routing flag) so PHI never hits the non-BAA Developer endpoint.

The classification.py catalog read is the single sanctioned Flask coupling: it reaches the DB through mrr_ai.catalog (Flask-SQLAlchemy db.session + implicit app context) and falls back to taxonomy constants when absent. Under FastAPI, re-seat this on a request-independent session accessor (the classifier runs on worker threads/processes, not inside a request), preserving the version-keyed cache + reset_catalog_cache invalidation seam.

Job pipeline externalization (Path B): job_queue.py's in-process ThreadPoolExecutor + single-writer Document.status machine + sweep_orphans boot reconciliation are the redesign target for Redis + worker processes. The services it calls (segment_engine.run_segmentation, summarize_engine, verify_pass) are already pure and take a progress(stage,current,total) callback - the callback just needs to write progress to Redis/DB from the worker instead of a Flask-scoped session. Workers must have Tesseract/Poppler + the sentence-transformers model available, and share the one Vertex quota pool (keep PIPELINE_WORKERS/CLASSIFY_WORKERS caps to avoid 429 storms).

Drop the entire classic surface as one unit: state.py globals, services/jobs.py, services/categorization.py, and the state-based blueprints - they encode the single-process, one-case-at-a-time design and the patient-named-path/OpenAI PHI anti-patterns the multi-user documents flow already replaced.

audit.py + logging need rewrite for the port: replace flask_security.current_user with the FastAPI auth principal, and introduce a structured logger whose formatter enforces the ids-only rule at the boundary (services currently print() freely). The DB writes stay against the same schema.

HIPAA re-review scope for the port: (1) PDF-at-rest under UPLOAD_FOLDER/<user_id>/<uuid>.pdf - keep uuid naming, confirm the FastAPI upload handler streams to disk without logging filenames; (2) Summary.source_text and SegmentRow fine-tuning PHI kept in the retained SQLite DB - protect DB-at-rest and export; (3) all four Vertex egress points gated on the BAA/Vertex flag; (4) confirm the local embedding path stays on-box on every worker; (5) verify no request/query-string or log line carries original_filename or OCR text end-to-end.
risks: The 'no Flask imports' purity is what makes ~10 services trivially portable; the classification->catalog DB read is the ONE crack. If the port does not re-seat that seam cleanly on a FastAPI/worker session, the classifier either loses admin-editable categories (silent fallback to taxonomy constants) or drags a Flask app context into worker processes.

PHI leaves the box at four Vertex call sites; the ONLY thing keeping that HIPAA-compliant is USE_VERTEX being true at runtime. It is a soft env flag with a Developer-API fallback (extensions L51, config L52-54) - a misconfigured deployment silently ships PHI to the non-BAA endpoint. The port should make Vertex non-optional in production and fail fast otherwise.

Externalizing the queue multiplies the Vertex quota contention surface: today PIPELINE_WORKERS x CLASSIFY_WORKERS is bounded in one process; independent worker processes each spin up their own pools and can storm the shared quota into sustained 429s. genai_retry backoff mitigates transient 429 but re-raises PerDay/free_tier - capacity planning is required.

Logging redaction is convention-only (scattered print() + an audit docstring), not an enforced sink. Any new log line in the port that includes original_filename, a state path, or OCR text is an undetected PHI leak; the classic prints of state.pdf_filepath show the pattern already exists.

files.py depends on werkzeug.secure_filename for path-traversal defense at the upload boundary. FastAPI/Starlette do not bundle werkzeug; dropping it without an equivalent sanitizer reopens a traversal vector on user-supplied filenames.

extensions.py runs validate_env() and builds clients at IMPORT time. Porting that import-time side effect into FastAPI naively can break test collection and worker bootstrapping; it should move to explicit startup/lifespan wiring.

prompts.py/taxonomy.py are seed + fallback, but the live source is the DB catalog. If the port re-seeds from constants on a DB that already holds admin edits, it must preserve seed idempotency (seed_catalog) or it will clobber curated categories/prompts.
openQuestions: Under the external queue, does classification's DB catalog read happen inside worker processes? If so, what session/connection do workers use against the shared SQLite file (WAL helps concurrent readers, but multi-process writers on SQLite are contention-prone) - is a move to Postgres/SQL Server implied for the multi-worker deployment despite the 'keep the same DB' goal?

Is the OpenAI legacy path (summarize.py/extraction.py + extensions.client) truly dead for the product, or does any user still rely on the classic CSV/individual-record summarize flow? Confirm before dropping OPENAI_API_KEY from REQUIRED_ENV.

Should Summary.source_text (raw OCR PHI stored for fine-tuning) continue to be persisted in the ported app, or is fine-tuning data collection out of scope for the re-platform (affects DB-at-rest HIPAA scope)?
For the Vertex client on workers: ADC via GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT (service-account) vs GEMINI_API_KEY - which auth mode is the production target, and are worker hosts provisioned for ADC?

Does the port need to preserve the progress(stage,current,total) callback contract exactly (segment/categorize/verify stages) for the Next.js UI's progress display, or will polling move to Redis job state with a different shape?

Are Tesseract + Poppler guaranteed present on the FastAPI hosts and every externalized worker, and is the local sentence-transformers model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) pre-provisioned there (first-use download is a cold-start + network dependency)?
